stories.headline: Iran war escalates as Israel strikes South Pars gasfield and kills intelligence minister; Iran retaliates against Gulf energy sites
stories.body: The Iran conflict entered a dangerous new phase as Israel struck Iran's South Pars gasfield, the world's largest natural gas reserve, and killed intelligence minister Esmail Khatib, the third senior Iranian figure eliminated in 24 hours. Iran retaliated by launching missiles at energy infrastructure across the Gulf, causing significant damage at Qatar's Ras Laffan facility, the world's largest LNG production site responsible for roughly 20 percent of global supply. Qatar declared Iranian embassy military attaches persona non grata and demanded they leave within 24 hours. Saudi Arabia's foreign minister warned that Gulf states' patience is 'not unlimited' and that they have 'very significant capabilities' to respond. Oil prices surged past $110 a barrel, with analysts no longer ruling out prices reaching $200 as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively blockaded. Canada is pushing for a joint G7 and Middle East de-escalation effort, with the Canadian foreign minister drafting a 'document of principles' to share with allies.

stories.headline: Global energy crisis deepens as Asian economies scramble to conserve fuel
stories.body: The Iran war's impact on global energy is rippling across Asia and beyond, with the International Energy Agency describing the situation as the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market. In Thailand, the government called on the public to reduce air conditioning use to save energy. The Philippines, which depends on the Gulf for 90 percent of its oil, has moved many government workers to a four-day week and introduced cash handouts for public transport drivers. Vietnam has urged employers to allow staff to work from home. Stock markets across Asia plunged following the latest attacks on Gulf energy facilities, while New Zealand faces questions about whether its fragile economic recovery can withstand the oil shock. Africa is also particularly vulnerable as the conflict disrupts supply chains across the continent.

stories.headline: Gabbard testifies Iran regime 'intact' but 'degraded'; Senate again blocks war powers resolution
stories.body: US Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard told the Senate that Iran's regime remains 'intact' though 'degraded' by the US-Israeli military campaign, while acknowledging that Iran was not rebuilding its nuclear enrichment capabilities prior to the start of the war. Gabbard refused to discuss private conversations with President Trump about the conflict. Meanwhile, Senate Republicans blocked for the second time a Democratic measure that would have required Trump to seek congressional authorization for the war, voting 53-47 along almost entirely party lines. The FBI is also investigating Joe Kent, the former counterterrorism director who resigned over the Iran war, reportedly angering Trump, who called him 'weak.'

stories.headline: Pakistan pauses Afghan airstrikes for Eid after deadly Kabul attack
stories.body: Pakistan announced it will pause airstrikes on Afghan targets during Eid, just two days after a devastating attack on a Kabul drug rehabilitation centre that Afghanistan says killed 400 people. The Pakistani military has been conducting strikes against Taliban targets inside Afghanistan in what it describes as a campaign to protect against terror attacks, a strategy that has drawn fierce condemnation from the Afghan government and international community. Witnesses described horrific scenes at the Kabul site, saying 'everything was burning, people were burning.' Pakistan's government hopes the steep cost of the strikes will deter future terror attacks on its soil.

stories.headline: Norway prosecutors seek over seven years in prison for crown princess's son in rape trial
stories.body: Norwegian prosecutors have requested more than seven years in prison for Marius Borg Hoiby, the son of Crown Princess Mette-Marit, in a rape trial that has gripped the Scandinavian nation. Borg Hoiby faces charges related to a sexual assault case that has become one of the most high-profile criminal proceedings involving European royalty in recent years. The case has put significant pressure on the Norwegian royal family, with the trial proceedings closely followed by media across the continent.

stories.headline: Cesar Chavez accused of sexually abusing Dolores Huerta and other women and girls
stories.body: A New York Times investigation has revealed allegations that Cesar Chavez, the iconic civil rights leader and co-founder of the United Farm Workers union, sexually abused women and girls for years during his leadership of the labor movement. Among those coming forward is Dolores Huerta, Chavez's longtime partner in the farmworker rights struggle, who revealed she was among the victims. The allegations have sent shockwaves through Latino communities across the country, with California Governor Gavin Newsom saying he is still 'processing' the revelations. Cesar Chavez Day events across the country are being renamed, postponed, or canceled in response to the accusations, and California's sex abuse laws could put the UFW at significant financial risk from resulting lawsuits.

stories.headline: Mullin confirmation hearing for DHS secretary turns combative
stories.body: Senator Markwayne Mullin's confirmation hearing to lead the Department of Homeland Security descended into heated exchanges, with the Trump ally presenting a markedly different vision for the department than his predecessor Kristi Noem. Mullin, who visited a war zone and made controversial claims about what he observed, faced pointed questions from senators on both sides of the aisle. Senator Rand Paul clashed sharply with Mullin during the proceedings, which one observer described as featuring 'one freaking snake and no apologies.' Mullin's approach to FEMA drew particular attention, as he outlined plans that sparked cautious hope among disaster response advocates for a more measured approach than the agency had seen under Noem.

stories.headline: Extreme weather batters half the US: blizzards, heat records, and Nebraska wildfire
stories.body: More than half the United States faced extreme weather conditions simultaneously, with blizzards hammering the northern plains and Midwest, a prolonged and unusual March heat wave gripping cities across the West, and a massive Nebraska wildfire dragging into its seventh day. In California, a community tied the all-time March temperature record for the entire country. Hazardous road condition warnings were issued across Minnesota, Michigan, and Wisconsin, where transportation officials warned of worsening conditions with low visibility and snow-covered roadways. The Nebraska wildfire, one of the largest in the state's history, saw easing winds but authorities warned that danger still looms.

stories.headline: Illinois primary results: Stratton wins Senate nod as AIPAC spending draws criticism
stories.body: Illinois Lieutenant Governor Juliana Stratton won the Democratic primary for US Senate, while Governor JB Pritzker criticized the pro-Israel lobbying group AIPAC for spending heavily in the state's primary races. The results came in a primary contest closely watched for how voters would respond to outside spending, particularly from groups tied to the Middle East conflict. Pritzker's public rebuke of AIPAC's involvement marked an unusual instance of a sitting governor from either party criticizing the influential organization's electoral spending.

stories.headline: Wyoming governor signs six-week abortion ban despite misgivings
stories.body: Wyoming's governor signed a ban on abortions after approximately six weeks of pregnancy, when cardiac activity can first be detected, while publicly expressing misgivings about the legislation. The signing came amid a patchwork of state-level abortion battles across the country. In Arizona, a judge struck down old abortion restrictions that clash with voter-backed constitutional guarantees, and California's governor said he would block Louisiana's attempt to extradite a doctor accused of mailing abortion pills. Nevada also paid $100,000 to a woman who had been convicted for a miscarriage under an 1911 law.

stories.headline: Sanchez shelves budget plans to focus on Iran crisis response package
stories.body: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez declared the Iran war 'one of the gravest crises of my mandate' and effectively postponed the presentation of the 2026 national budget to focus on an emergency economic response package to be approved at an extraordinary cabinet meeting on Friday. The package has become a source of friction among coalition partners, with Sumar's health minister calling it 'inconceivable' that housing measures are excluded, while the PNV warned that including them would be 'playing Russian roulette' with the vote. EH Bildu and ERC are also pushing for rental protections to be included. Sanchez emphasized that Spain's energy policy has been validated by the crisis but declined to specify which measures would ultimately make it into the decree.

stories.headline: Vox drops sharply in polls after backing Trump's Iran war
stories.body: The CIS March barometer showed Vox losing 2.3 percentage points in estimated vote share, falling to 16.6 percent, its worst result since July 2025, as the party pays a political price for its unconditional support of Donald Trump and the US-Israeli war on Iran. The PSOE would lead with 31.8 percent, more than eight points ahead of the PP at 23.3 percent. The survey, conducted during the first week of March when Spain refused to allow US forces to use its Rota and Moron bases, found that nearly seven in ten Spaniards reject the US and Israeli military actions in Iran. Overall, 81.4 percent of respondents expressed concern about the conflict and 85.4 percent consider it a significant risk to international peace.

stories.headline: Illa withdraws Catalan budget to renegotiate with ERC
stories.body: Catalan President Salvador Illa withdrew the regional government's 2026 budget from parliamentary proceedings to buy time for renegotiations with his key investiture ally, ERC. The decision followed intensive talks between Illa and ERC leader Oriol Junqueras, with both parties issuing a joint statement committing to 'preserve the country's stability at a particularly turbulent moment.' The government will approve a supplementary credit to guarantee public services while negotiations continue. PSC and ERC pledged to continue working on the investiture agreements, including ERC's demand on income tax reform, aiming to approve the budget before the parliamentary summer recess.

stories.headline: Spain to evacuate soldiers from Iraq as Middle East conflict worsens
stories.body: Defense Minister Margarita Robles confirmed that Spain is preparing the evacuation and relocation of its military personnel deployed in Iraq, as part of a NATO decision to adjust the mission for security reasons. Spain contributes a 71-member special operations tactical group to the anti-ISIS coalition, along with headquarters staff and Iraqi army training personnel. The troops had already been temporarily relocated over the weekend as security conditions deteriorated due to Iran's bombardment of the Green Zone in Baghdad and other areas of Iraq. Robles said the full evacuation would be carried out in the coming days.

stories.headline: Prosecutors seek 173 years in prison for former BBVA president over Villarejo spying case
stories.body: Spain's anti-corruption prosecutors have requested 173 years in prison for Francisco Gonzalez, the former president of banking giant BBVA, for contracting former police commissioner Jose Manuel Villarejo to spy on various businesspeople over more than a decade. The extraordinary sentence request stems from the accumulation of individual charges for the revelation of secrets of each person surveilled. The case is part of the sprawling Tandem investigation into Villarejo's activities, with the National Court having confirmed proceedings against BBVA and its former president for allegedly illegal activities carried out between 2004 and 2016, involving a total of fourteen defendants including bank executives and police commanders.

stories.headline: Search intensifies for American student Jimmy Gracey who vanished in Barcelona
stories.body: The Mossos d'Esquadra have opened an investigation into the disappearance of Jimmy Gracey, a 20-year-old University of Alabama student from Elmhurst, Illinois, who was last seen in Barcelona's Vila Olimpica neighborhood in the early hours of Tuesday, March 17. Gracey was visiting friends in Barcelona during his university spring break. His mother has made a public appeal on Facebook asking for help locating her son, and the case is generating significant concern in the United States. Police confirmed the missing person report was filed on Tuesday night and the investigation is ongoing.

stories.headline: Teacher strikes continue across Catalunya with protests in Lleida, Manresa, and La Seu d'Urgell
stories.body: The third day of rolling education strikes hit schools across the Catalunya Central, Lleida, and the Alt Pirineu i Aran regions on Wednesday, with teachers, professors, and support staff walking off the job. The Department of Education reported 29 percent strike participation based on data from 82 percent of schools, while the USTEC union claimed 65 percent participation, denouncing that minimum service requirements for rural schools effectively prevented many teachers from joining. Protesters staged demonstrations in Lleida, Manresa, and La Seu d'Urgell after early morning road blockades. Doctors also joined strike actions on the same day, with minimum services announced for Thursday and Friday across Catalunya.

stories.headline: Barcelona unveils 22 million euro plan to revitalize the Paral-lel
stories.body: The Barcelona city government announced a 22 million euro plan to revitalize the Avinguda del Paral-lel, the avenue historically associated with theaters and variety shows, aiming to boost its commercial, cultural, and neighborhood life while improving urban connections between surrounding districts. The plan bundles existing projects, including the renovation of the historic Teatre Arnau and the reurbanization of the Tres Xemeneies park plaza, with new interventions along the southern stretch between Ronda Sant Pau and Drassanes. At the upper end, a separate longer-term operation envisions transforming Placa Espanya by 2035 with 500 affordable housing units and new public facilities.

stories.headline: Sant Jordi book and rose stalls to move from Rambla to Gothic Quarter due to construction
stories.body: Barcelona's iconic Sant Jordi celebrations will take an unprecedented detour this year as the book and rose stalls traditionally lining the Rambla are relocated to the Gothic Quarter because of ongoing major construction works on the boulevard. The temporary route will create a new circuit connecting Placa de Catalunya to the Avinguda de la Catedral through the heart of the old city. In previous years, the Sant Jordi epicenter was the vertical axis formed by Passeig de Gracia's literary superblock and the Rambla, drawing tens of thousands of visitors. Last year the stalls could remain because only part of the road was under construction, but the current phase of works makes it impossible to accommodate sellers among the excavations.

stories.headline: Barcelona launches tender for 625 affordable rental homes on municipal land
stories.body: The Barcelona city council opened a public tender for five municipal plots to build and manage 625 affordable rental housing units, open to both private sector companies and nonprofit organizations. Three plots will be offered under a 75-year surface rights agreement, extendable to 90 years, while two will be offered as public domain concessions for 50 years, extendable to 75. The move comes as the city also considers a broader economic response plan to address rising costs from the Middle East conflict, with opposition parties Barcelona en Comu, ERC, and Junts all pushing for measures to help residents, small businesses, and the self-employed cope with price increases.
